2605.03907.title: Steer Like the LLM: Activation Steering that Mimics Prompting
2605.03907.paper_summary: Large language models can be steered at inference time through prompting or activation interventions, but activation steering methods often underperform compared to prompt-based approaches. We propose a framework that formulates prompt steering as a form of activation steering and investigates whether distilling successful prompt steering behavior into simpler, interpretable models can close this gap. Our analysis reveals that popular activation steering methods are not faithful to the mechanics of prompt steering, which applies strong interventions on some tokens while barely affecting others. Based on these insights, we introduce Prompt Steering Replacement (PSR) models that estimate token-specific steering coefficients from the activations themselves and are trained to imitate prompt-based interventions. Experiments on three steering benchmarks across multiple language models show that PSR models outperform existing activation steering methods, especially when controlling for high-coherence completions, and also compare favorably to prompting on AxBench and persona steering.
2605.03907.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型可以透過提示或激活干預在推理時進行引導，但激活引導方法通常表現不如基於提示的方法。我們提出一個框架，將提示引導形式化為一種激活引導，並探討將成功的提示引導行為提煉成更簡單、可解釋的模型是否能縮小這一差距。我們的分析顯示，流行的激活引導方法並未忠實於提示引導的機制，後者對某些標記施加強烈的干預，而對其他標記幾乎沒有影響。基於這些見解，我們引入了提示引導替代（PSR）模型，該模型從激活本身估算特定標記的引導係數，並被訓練以模仿基於提示的干預。在多個語言模型的三個引導基準上的實驗顯示，PSR模型的表現超過了現有的激活引導方法，特別是在控制高一致性完成時，並且在AxBench和角色引導上也與提示相比具有良好的表現。
2605.03907.author: Geert Heyman et.al.
